items.title: Stablecoin flows and spillovers to FX markets
items.desc: by Iñaki Aldasoro, Paula Beltrán, Federico GrinbergUsing data on four USD-pegged stablecoins and 27 fiat currencies, this paper documents spillovers from stablecoin-based foreign exchange (FX) to traditional FXmarkets. We document a gap between the cost of acquiring dollars via stablecoins and via the spot FX market (parity deviations). To establish a causal link between stablecoin flows and FX markets, we use a granular instrumental variable that exploits idiosyncratic shocks to stablecoin net inflows in other currencies.

items.title: Japan’s SBI, Rakuten developing crypto investment trusts in-house: Nikkei
items.desc: The push comes after Japan's cabinet approved a bill last month that would reclassify crypto under the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act.

items.title: The Clarity Act took a step forward: State of Crypto
items.desc: Unpacking Thursday's at-times contentious markup hearing.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.desc: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: DeFi's new front: VerifiedX bets bitcoin's next chapter is programmable, private
items.desc: VerifiedX says its Bitcoin sidechain enables programmable, privacy-preserving transactions without synthetic wrappers, targeting growing institutional demand for native DeFi on the original blockchain system.

items.title: Japan's SBI Securities, Rakuten Securities plan to offer crypto investment trusts
items.desc: Another 11 companies responded to a survey saying they would consider offering crypto funds once the regulatory environment becomes clear.

items.title: SBI, Rakuten, Nomura line up to launch crypto investment trusts: Report
items.desc: Japan’s biggest brokerages are racing to bring crypto investment trusts to retail investors, as regulators move to formally allow crypto-holding funds by 2028.

items.title: Bitcoin slides below $79K on macro fears: Can fixed-income outflows save it?
items.desc: While macro pain and Iran war uncertainty drag Bitcoin below $79K, fixed-income market outflows could trigger a medium-term Bitcoin rebound.

items.title: Italy’s largest bank more than doubles crypto holdings to $235M in Q1: Report
items.desc: Intesa Sanpaolo grew its crypto holdings from $100 million to $235 million in Q1 2026, making first-time moves into Ethereum and XRP while nearly exiting Solana entirely.

items.title: A Russian stablecoin built to dodge sanctions says it can survive even if they're lifted
items.desc: A7A5, the Russia-linked stablecoin built to move money around banking restrictions, says faster trade settlement, yield and regional crypto infrastructure could keep it relevant even if geopolitical tensions ease.

items.title: Michael Saylor floated Bitcoin sales idea to avoid 'impairing' the asset
items.desc: Strategy executive chairman Michael Saylor said that continuing to use the “never sell” Bitcoin mantra could ultimately undermine the very asset his company is built around.

items.title: US CLARITY Act will be a ‘boon for domestic innovation’: A16z
items.desc: A16z crypto said that when US legal frameworks strike a balance between innovation and consumer protection, the impact tends to ripple through into global markets.

items.title: VanEck, Grayscale file fresh BNB ETF amendments as race for next altcoin spot ETF accelerates
items.desc: Canary Capital, meanwhile, continues to push forward on its first-of-its-kind staked TRX ETF, also filing an amendment on Friday.

items.title: Strategy to repurchase $1.5B of 2029 convertible notes
items.desc: The notes carry a 0% coupon, but can be converted into equity in the company if holders choose to redeem their notes for shares.

items.title: Justin Sun-Led Liberland Micronation Awards Ethereum Founder Vitalik Buterin Its Top Honor
items.desc: The micronation honored Vitalik Buterin during ETH Prague 2026 as it continued promoting blockchain-based governance and digital citizenship.

items.title: Harvard dumps ether ETF as Abu Dhabi sovereign fund keeps adding to bitcoin positions
items.desc: Abu Dhabi-based Mubadala added over $90 million to its already sizeable position in BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF.

items.title: Bitcoin analysis sees 'bear trap' as BTC price passes two-week lows under $78K
items.desc: Bitcoin fell below $78,000 for the first time since the start of May, but traders refused to give up hope of a BTC price rebound coming next.

items.title: Jump Crypto’s ‘Firedancer’ is taking a slow and steady approach to its long-awaited Solana infrastructure rollout
items.desc: In an interview with CoinDesk, the lead engineer at Firedancer gives an update on how the new client, also known as a software, is fairing in the Solana ecosystem.

items.title: Crypto users are choosing juicy yields over protection, putting billions at risk of hacks
items.desc: DeFi insurance protocols debuted with huge ambitions during the 2020 crypto boom. But as hacks evolved and users chased yields over protection, most of the sector collapsed under the same risks it was built to cover.

items.title: The $293 million KelpDAO hack shows why DeFi is finally being forced to grow up
items.desc: For protocol founders and security researchers, the incident reinforced a broader shift underway across crypto: DeFi is no longer primarily battling coding bugs. It’s battling complexity.

items.title: XRP beat bitcoin gains as Clarity Act advanced, but a real bullrun still needs Congress
items.desc: The token jumped 5% after a Senate committee moved the market-structure bill forward, reviving hopes that legal clarity can pull deeper institutional money into XRP products.

items.title: Sponsor reviews crypto conference partnership over pole-dancing party
items.desc: Crypto exchange OKX reconsiders sponsorship of Consensus Miami after ‘immature’ official after-party event

items.title: THORChain confirms $10M exploit, rolls out recovery portal for affected users
items.desc: THORChain has launched a recovery portal following a $10 million exploit, allowing affected users across four chains to revoke malicious approvals and claim refunds.

items.title: Bhutan ‘doesn’t recall’ selling any bitcoin, disputing widely tracked $1 billion BTC drawdown
items.desc: Arkham Intelligence data shows that over $1 billion in bitcoin has left wallets attributed to Bhutan in the past year, flowing to exchanges and trading firms. The country says it has not sold any.

items.title: US CLARITY Act brings ‘major spike of euphoria’ to Bitcoin: Santiment
items.desc: Santiment warned that the crypto market “typically” moves against crowd expectations, pointing to a recent rise in bullish sentiment driven by renewed momentum around the US CLARITY Act.

items.title: Bitcoin Depot filing casts doubt on company’s future amid lawsuits
items.desc: The crypto ATM company reported financial difficulties amid a changing regulatory environment and ongoing litigation, which have cost it millions of dollars.

items.title: Revolut Adds Employee Incentives to Fuel Business Banking Push
items.desc: Revolut is offering employees across all departments 1,000 pounds (about $1,330) if they bring businesses to the digital bank as it expands its business banking offerings, Bloomberg reported Friday (May 15), citing a memo sent to staff by Revolut CEO Nik Storonsky. Storonsky also said in the memo that the FinTech company plans to […] The post Revolut Adds Employee Incentives to Fuel Business Banking Push appeared first on PYMNTS.com.

items.title: ICE, CME press US regulators to 'rein in' Hyperliquid energy trading: Report
items.desc: The Hyperliquid decentralized exchange allows anyone who stakes 500,000 HYPE tokens, valued at roughly $22.2 million, to deploy new markets.

items.title: Solayer launches Visa-compatible card for USDC payments
items.desc: The new card lets users spend USDC balances through online, in-store and contactless transactions while accessing ATM withdrawals in supported regions.

items.title: Drake Calls for Sam Bankman-Fried's Release in New, Critically Panned Album
items.desc: Rapper Drake released three new albums on Friday, and described himself on one as “a BTC crypto big-timer.”

items.title: Bitcoin Open Heads to Iconic Glen Abbey Golf Club for June 8, 2026 Event
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoin Open Heads to Iconic Glen Abbey Golf Club for June 8, 2026 Event Organizers of the Bitcoin Open have confirmed the June 8, 2026 date at Glen Abbey Golf Club in Oakville, Ontario, during the venue’s 50th anniversary year. The event combines a team scramble golf tournament with an evening Texas Hold’em poker tournament. This post Bitcoin Open Heads to Iconic Glen Abbey Golf Club for June 8, 2026 Event first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Juan Galt.

items.title: Lombard Finance Dumps LayerZero, Will Use Chainlink to Power $1 Billion in Bitcoin Assets
items.desc: The Bitcoin DeFi protocol is moving its assets away from LayerZero following the Kelp DAO exploit that led to the loss of $292 million.

items.title: Crypto market structure bill still faces significant hurdles despite Senate committee win: analysts
items.desc: TD Cowen raised the probability of the bill passing to 40% from 33% while Benchmark said the Clarity Act will need more Democratic support.

items.title: It’s a ‘new era’ for crypto users who ‘trade anything and everything’ as Jito goes consumer, CEO Bruder says
items.desc: Jito founder Lucas Bruder told The Block that there's a new class of users coming onchain who "want to trade anything and everything."

items.title: Hyperliquid Policy Arm Rejects Market Integrity Concerns Amid Oil Futures Surge
items.desc: Decentralized exchange Hyperliquid has become a popular destination for speculating on oil prices.

items.title: CVE-2024-52911 - Script Interpreter Remote Crash
items.desc: After Bitcoin Core 0.14.0 and before Bitcoin Core 29.0, validating a specially-crafted block may cause the node to access previously freed memory. During validation, necessary data required for checking inputs for each transaction is pre-calculated and cached. For specially crafted invalid blocks, it was possible for this data to be destroyed while it was still being accessed by a background validation thread. An attacker capable of mining a block with sufficient proof-of-work could have exploited this to crash victim nodes. Because of the nature of use-after-free bugs, it is possible that the crash could have been used for remote code execution, though constraints on the input (block) data make this unlikely. This issue is considered High severity. Details By default, script validation for new blocks is dispatched to background threads via a vector of CScriptCheck functors. Each CScriptCheck holds a pointer to a PrecomputedTransactionData object which stores some data needed by each input in the transaction. Because it stores a pointer and not the data itself, care must be taken to ensure that the PrecomputedTransactionData outlives the CScriptCheck. The script checks lifetime is enforced by an RAII class, CCheckQueueControl. However, the control is intantiated before the precomputed transaction data. Because local objects in C++ are destructed in reverse order of construction, this means the vector of PrecomputedTransactionData is destroyed before the CCheckQueueControl. This is not an issue when the block is valid, as CCheckQueueControl::Wait() will be called before the function returns and the PrecomputedTransactionData gets destroyed. However, in case of an early return (when a separate check fails) a background script thread may read the precomputed transaction data after it was destroyed. An attacker could exploit this to crash victim nodes at the expense of a valid PoW at tip. Attribution Cory Fields (MIT DCI) discovered this vulnerability and responsibly disclosed it in a detailed report containing a proof of concept for reproduction and a proposed mitigation. Timeline 2024-11-02 Cory Fields privately reports the bug 2024-11-06 Pieter Wuille pushes a covert fix to already open PR #31112 which works around the issue by removing the early returns 2024-12-03 PR #31112 is merged 2025-04-12 Bitcoin Core version 29.0 is released with a fix 2026-04-19 The last vulnerable Bitcoin Core version (28.x) goes end of life 2026-05-05 Public disclosure.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
items.desc: Bitcoin Core version 31.0 is now available for download. See the release notes for more information about the bug fixes in this release. If you have any questions, please stop by the #bitcoin IRC chatroom (IRC, web) and we’ll do our best to help you.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
